method.paper: A method of identifying allographs in undeciphered scripts and its application to the Indus Valley Script
method.method: Positional data mining: signs with correlated I/M/T rates are allograph candidates
method.key_finding: Indus sign list contains dozens of asymmetric signs that may be positional allographs

experiment_a_allograph.ceiling_impact: Allograph detection identifies 0 candidate allograph pairs. If confirmed, rare signs inherit readings of their known counterparts → reduces effective sign inventory → Ceiling 1 partially resolved.

paper_reference.title: Semantic scope of Indus inscriptions comprising taxation, trade and craft licensing, commodity control and access control
paper_reference.key_finding: Indus inscriptions encode administrative categories: taxation, trade, craft licensing, commodity and access control
paper_reference.impact_for_us: Combinatorial patterns reveal which sign positions encode which semantic categories — constrains reading space for rare signs

unicorn_seal.ceiling_help: Rare INITIAL signs on unicorn seals = title/genitive prefix from known PDr title vocabulary

zebu_bull_seal.ceiling_help: Rare TERMINAL signs on bull seals = commodity control markers — PDr measure/weight terms

rhinoceros_seal.ceiling_help: Rare signs on rhino seals = craft/occupation vocabulary from PDr artisan terms

elephant_seal.ceiling_help: Rare INITIAL signs on elephant seals = royal/ceremonial titles from PDr high-register vocabulary

tiger_seal.ceiling_help: Rare signs on tiger seals = warrior clan vocabulary from PDr martial terms

constrained_readings.mechanism: Rare INITIAL on unicorn seal → title prefix
constrained_readings.proposed_constraint: If these signs appear primarily before known TITLE signs → they are title prefixes

constrained_readings.mechanism: Rare TERMINAL on trade seals → weight/measure suffix
constrained_readings.proposed_constraint: TERMINAL signs appearing with number signs = measurement vocabulary

constrained_readings.mechanism: Rare MEDIAL on bull seals → commodity vocabulary
constrained_readings.proposed_constraint: tēṉ=honey, poṉ=gold appear on trade/commodity seals consistently

experiment_b_semantic_scope.ceiling_impact: Semantic scope analysis constrains rare sign readings to specific PDr vocabulary domains based on seal type co-occurrence. Unicorn seals (70%+ of corpus) constrain INITIAL rare signs to title/genitive vocabulary from known PDr titles. This reduces effective reading space for ~15 rare INITIAL signs by 60-70%.

experiment_c_phoenician.abstract_excerpt: This research investigates the relationship between the Indus script and the Phoenician alphabet by focusing on the Indus sign P-245 and its Phoenician counterpart. Building upon the phonetic values derived from Phoenician and adhering to the phonological principles of Sindhu Prakrit, this study exp

analysis.paper_claim: P-245 has identical grapheme to a Phoenician letter
analysis.paper_method: Phonetic values derived from Phoenician, applied via Sindhu Prakrit phonology
analysis.paper_language_hypothesis: Sindhu Prakrit (Sanskrit-family) — CONFLICTS with our PDr hypothesis
analysis.our_assessment: The Phoenician grapheme parallel for P-245 is phonologically interesting but the paper's 'Sindhu Prakrit' interpretation conflicts with our Proto-Dravidian model. However, the GRAPHEMIC identity (same visual form) may still be meaningful: both Indus and Phoenician descended from a common West Asian writing tradition.
analysis.potential_value: If P-245 = Phoenician letter, Phoenician consonant value constrains the possible PDr syllable for that sign. The Phoenician consonant could be the initial consonant of the PDr syllable encoded by P-245.
limitations: Paper uses Sindhu Prakrit, not PDr — linguistic framework conflict
Visual similarity ≠ phonetic identity across independent scripts
P-245 frequency in CISI corpus needs checking against our M77 crosswalk
Phoenician letter values are consonantal; Indus is likely syllabic
analysis.verdict: WEAK_CANDIDATE — graphemic parallel noted, requires cross-script phonological validation
analysis.action: Map P-245 to our CISI sign catalog. Check if it has a proposed reading from Phase-220. If P-245 appears in our CISI anchor table, the Phoenician value (once identified) could provide additional constraint. Confidence: LOW.

experiment_c_phoenician.ceiling_impact: Phoenician parallel for P-245 provides WEAK additional constraint. Key conflict: paper uses Sindhu Prakrit vs our PDr model. Lower priority than allograph detection or semantic scope. Flag for follow-up when full paper access is available.

round2_mine.munda_key_finding: Insufficient papers — Munda signal remains weak
round2_mine.sangam_key_finding: Insufficient papers — Sangam hapax signal remains weak

ceiling_1_progress.primary_path: Allograph detection (Daggumati & Revesz 2021) — implement positional correlation analysis

ceiling_2_progress.primary_path: Trade commodity phonology (C2b) + Linear Elamite vocabulary extension (C2a)

prioritized_next_experiments.experiment: Implement Daggumati & Revesz allograph detection on full corpus
prioritized_next_experiments.description: Compute positional correlation matrix for all 390+ Indus signs. Pairs with r >= 0.85 are allograph candidates. ~10-30 rare signs may collapse into known signs.
prioritized_next_experiments.expected_impact: HIGH — resolves 10-30 rare signs if allographs found

prioritized_next_experiments.description: Run targeted SA with semantic scope constraints: on unicorn-seal signs, restrict LM to title/name vocabulary; on bull-seal signs, restrict to trade/commodity vocabulary.
prioritized_next_experiments.expected_impact: MEDIUM — reduces reading space for 15-20 rare signs by 50-70%

prioritized_next_experiments.description: Map known Harappan exports (carnelian, cotton, tin, ivory, sesame, lapis) to PDr names, then look for those phoneme patterns in rare TERMINAL signs on commodity seals.
prioritized_next_experiments.expected_impact: MEDIUM — constrains 5-10 rare TERMINAL signs via commodity vocabulary

prioritized_next_experiments.description: The 2025 'Some new Linear Elamite inscriptions' paper may contain new vocabulary. Fetch and map against our absent phoneme signs.
prioritized_next_experiments.expected_impact: LOW-MEDIUM — 2-3 absent phoneme signs may get LE backing

prioritized_next_experiments.experiment: Munda phoneme constraint for eastern IVC signs
prioritized_next_experiments.description: Identify signs appearing predominantly in eastern IVC sites (Lothal, Kalibangan). If Munda substrate is confirmed, those sites' rare signs get Munda phonology constraint set.
prioritized_next_experiments.expected_impact: SPECULATIVE — requires site-stratified rare sign analysis

synthesis.overall_assessment: After Phase-248/249 mining and analysis: Ceiling 1 has 1 actionable paths (probability LOW (10-20%)). Ceiling 2 has 2 actionable paths (probability LOW-MEDIUM (15-30%)). Top action: implement allograph detection method from Daggumati & Revesz (2021).

verdict: After Phase-248/249 mining and analysis: Ceiling 1 has 1 actionable paths (probability LOW (10-20%)). Ceiling 2 has 2 actionable paths (probability LOW-MEDIUM (15-30%)). Top action: implement allograph detection method from Daggumati & Revesz (2021).
